Row 45: ...cWWg... with RRRR at col 4, 20, 36

- 9 outer blocks (3x3 arrangement)
- 1 center cell with a complex pattern (gray/white around a red 2x2 square)

Row 48: ...gggg... - this is part of Grid 3's center cell
Row 49: ...gggc... - this is part of Grid 3's center cell
Wait, the RRRR at col 4 is the red block at (1,0) of the left grids. The center cell is to the right of that.
Let me look at the ascii more carefully. The center cell of Grid 3 is at rows 44-49, and it has:
Row 44: ccWW... (cols 0-5)
Row 45: cWWg...
Row 46: WWRR...
Row 47: WRRW...
Row 48: gggg...
Row 49: gggc...
So the center cell of Grid 3 is at cols 0-5 (or maybe 2-7). The pattern is:
ccWW
cWWg
WWRR
WRRW
gggg
gggc
Hmm, this is getting confusing. Let me try a different approach. Let me use the segmentation to find the center cells.
